verses.text: A Levite's son is hidden three months, then laid in an ark of bulrushes by the river, his sister watching. Hidden child. Faith shelters the babe from the king's wrath.
A Levite's son is hidden three months, then laid in an ark of bulrushes by the river, his sister watching. Hidden child. Faith shelters the babe from the king's wrath.
A Levite's son is hidden three months, then laid in an ark of bulrushes by the river, his sister watching. Hidden child. Faith shelters the babe from the king's wrath.
A Levite's son is hidden three months, then laid in an ark of bulrushes by the river, his sister watching. Hidden child. Faith shelters the babe from the king's wrath.
Pharaoh's daughter finds him weeping and has compassion: 'This is one of the Hebrews' children.' Drawn out. God's providence works through a princess's pity.
Pharaoh's daughter finds him weeping and has compassion: 'This is one of the Hebrews' children.' Drawn out. God's providence works through a princess's pity.
The sister fetches the babe's own mother to nurse him for wages. Sweet turn. God restores the child to his mother and pays her too.
The sister fetches the babe's own mother to nurse him for wages. Sweet turn. God restores the child to his mother and pays her too.
The sister fetches the babe's own mother to nurse him for wages. Sweet turn. God restores the child to his mother and pays her too.
He is named Moses, 'Because I drew him out of the water.' Named. The deliverer is reared in the very court that doomed his people.
Grown, Moses sees an Egyptian smiting a Hebrew and 'slew the Egyptian, and hid him in the sand.' Rash zeal. He acts for his people, but in his own strength.
Grown, Moses sees an Egyptian smiting a Hebrew and 'slew the Egyptian, and hid him in the sand.' Rash zeal. He acts for his people, but in his own strength.
Rebuking a Hebrew, he is exposed; Pharaoh seeks his life, and Moses flees to Midian. Exposed and fled. The would-be deliverer becomes a fugitive.
Rebuking a Hebrew, he is exposed; Pharaoh seeks his life, and Moses flees to Midian. Exposed and fled. The would-be deliverer becomes a fugitive.
Rebuking a Hebrew, he is exposed; Pharaoh seeks his life, and Moses flees to Midian. Exposed and fled. The would-be deliverer becomes a fugitive.
At a well he helps the daughters of Midian's priest, driving off the shepherds. Helper. Even in exile he defends the wronged.
At a well he helps the daughters of Midian's priest, driving off the shepherds. Helper. Even in exile he defends the wronged.
Reuel hears of it and calls Moses in to eat bread. Welcomed. Kindness opens a home to the stranger.
Reuel hears of it and calls Moses in to eat bread. Welcomed. Kindness opens a home to the stranger.
Reuel hears of it and calls Moses in to eat bread. Welcomed. Kindness opens a home to the stranger.
Moses dwells there, weds Zipporah, and names his son Gershom, 'a stranger in a strange land.' Sojourner. He waits, unknowing, for God's time.
Moses dwells there, weds Zipporah, and names his son Gershom, 'a stranger in a strange land.' Sojourner. He waits, unknowing, for God's time.
Israel groans under bondage; 'God heard their groaning... and God had respect unto them.' God remembers. He hears His people's cry and prepares deliverance.
